name: image.read
summary: Deeply analyze an image file: describe it, transcribe all visible text, interpret diagrams/charts. Optionally answer a question about it.

path.description: Path to the image file to analyze

question.description: Optional question to answer about the image

requires: a multimodal backend (Claude) for full understanding, or OCR (mac Vision / Windows OCR / tesseract) for text

name: image.generate
summary: Generate an image from a text prompt (free, no API key) and save it to a file.

prompt.description: What to draw, e.g. 'a watercolor fox in a snowy forest'

out.description: Output path (must be under the OS temp dir or workspace). Default: temp/helm-image-<ts>.jpg

side_effects: writes a generated image file; to show it, end the reply with `ATTACH: <path>`

name: screencap
summary: Capture a screenshot of the Mac screen to a temp file.

name: gui.click
summary: Click at point coordinates on screen (points, not pixels).

name: gui.type
summary: Type text into the currently focused element.

name: gui.key
summary: Press a key by macOS key code with optional modifiers.

name: imessage.send
summary: Send an iMessage to a handle (phone number or email).

name: discord.attach
summary: Attach a file to the Discord reply by appending ATTACH: /path to stdout.
side_effects: prints ATTACH: line; Discord bot handles the rest

name: memory.remember
summary: Store a structured fact in memory.db.

name: memory.recall
summary: Query memory.db by keywords; returns ranked facts.

name: scheduler.add
summary: Register a new scheduled job in jobs.db.

cron.description: 5-field cron: min hour dom month dow

notify.description: DM owner with the job result when it finishes. Set false for silent housekeeping jobs.

name: scheduler.list
summary: List all scheduled jobs.

name: browser.open
summary: Open a URL in headless Chromium and return page text. Logins persist via workspace/browser-profile/.
requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use via npx playwright install chromium
side_effects: saves current URL to workspace/browser-state.json; may write cookies to browser-profile/

name: browser.read
summary: Read text content of the current browser page (set by the last browser.open or browser.click).

name: browser.click
summary: Click a CSS selector on the current page; follows navigation if it occurs.

name: browser.fill
summary: Fill a form input on the current page with text.
side_effects: modifies form field on loaded page

name: browser.screenshot
summary: Screenshot the current browser page viewport.

name: browser.close
summary: Clear the current browser session state (forgets current URL; cookies in profile persist).

name: browser.images
summary: Navigate to a URL in Chromium (reusing any saved login), scroll to load lazy images, and download the page's images — ranked biggest-first — to a folder. No site API needed.

url.description: Page to pull images from, e.g. https://www.facebook.com/<page>/photos

min.description: Skip images smaller than this many bytes (drops tracking pixels / tiny icons)

scroll.description: Scroll passes to trigger lazy / infinite-scroll loading (0-60)

headful.description: Show the browser window while scraping (default: invisible/headless)

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use
For login-walled sites (Facebook, Instagram, X): run browser.login once first
side_effects: writes image files under workspace/downloads/; outbound requests to the page and its image hosts. To show one in chat, end the reply with `ATTACH: <path>`

name: browser.login
summary: Open a URL in a VISIBLE browser window so the owner can sign in once. The session cookies persist in the browser profile, so later browser.open / browser.images calls are already logged in. Use for Facebook, Instagram, X, etc.

seconds.description: How long to keep the window open for sign-in (10-900). Closing the window finishes early.

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use
A visible/unlocked desktop (the owner needs to interact with the window)
side_effects: opens a real browser window; saves login cookies into workspace/browser-profile/ (gitignored, private)

name: imessage.send_to
summary: Send an iMessage to any handle (phone or email). First-class proactive send — not a reply.

handle.description: Phone number (+66...) or Apple ID email

side_effects: sends a real iMessage to the specified handle

name: calendar.list
summary: List upcoming calendar events via Calendar.app (JXA). Falls back gracefully if Calendar is inaccessible.

days.description: How many days ahead to look

name: calendar.add
summary: Add an event to the first writable calendar via Calendar.app.

name: finder.search
summary: Search for files by name or content using mdfind (Spotlight).

name: finder.reveal
summary: Reveal a file or folder in Finder (brings Finder to front).
side_effects: opens Finder, brings window to front

name: web.fetch
summary: Fetch a URL with curl and return stripped plain text. No API key required.

name: web.search
summary: Search the web via DuckDuckGo HTML endpoint. Returns titles, URLs, snippets. No API key.

name: screen.recent
summary: Return last N screen-change events (timestamp, hash, path). Requires screen watcher daemon running.

name: screen.at
summary: Return the screen event closest to a given Unix timestamp (ms).

name: screen.search
summary: Search OCR text from screen events. Only useful when watcher was started with --ocr flag.

name: notify.recent
summary: Return last N notification events (Messages/Calendar/Mail) recorded by the notify poller daemon.

name: notify.unread
summary: Return current unread totals: { messages, calendar, mail }. Queries live; poller not required.
requires: Full Disk Access (Messages), Automation > Calendar, Automation > Mail (all optional; missing returns null)

name: location.here
summary: Return current location { lat, lon, accuracy, ts } via CoreLocationCLI, or { installed: false } if not installed.
requires: CoreLocationCLI (brew install corlocationcli — must ask owner first)

name: mic.record
summary: Record a WAV clip from the default microphone. Uses sox or ffmpeg. Returns path to WAV or install note.
requires: sox (brew install sox) or ffmpeg (brew install ffmpeg) — ask owner before installing

name: mic.transcribe
summary: Transcribe a WAV file using whisper.cpp. Returns { text } or install note if whisper.cpp missing.
requires: whisper.cpp (brew install whisper-cpp — ask owner before installing)

name: vision.describe
summary: Take a screenshot and use Claude to describe what is visible on screen.

out.description: Output path for temp screenshot PNG. Default: /tmp/helm-vision.png

scale.description: Retina display scale factor (pixels÷scale = points). Default: 2

side_effects: writes temp PNG; makes a Claude API call via claude -p

name: vision.find
summary: Take a screenshot and use Claude to locate a UI element; returns its center {x,y} in points for gui.click.

query.description: Description of the UI element to find, e.g. 'Submit button'

out.description: Output path for temp screenshot PNG. Default: /tmp/helm-vision.png

scale.description: Retina display scale factor (pixels÷scale = points). Default: 2

side_effects: writes temp PNG; makes a Claude API call via claude -p

name: vision.verify
summary: Take a screenshot and ask Claude whether an expected screen state is currently visible. Use after gui.click or gui.type to confirm the action worked.

expect.description: Natural-language description of what should be visible, e.g. 'the text field contains hello'

out.description: Output path for temp screenshot PNG. Default: /tmp/helm-vision.png

side_effects: writes temp PNG; makes a Claude API call via claude -p

name: gui.step
summary: Run a shell command, screenshot, verify with Claude that the action succeeded, retry up to N times with failure classification.

cmd.description: Shell command to execute as the GUI action

description.description: Natural-language description of what success looks like on screen

side_effects: executes cmd, writes temp screenshots, makes Claude API calls via claude -p

name: reverse.web
summary: Reverse-engineer a web URL (cross-platform): detect stack, capture live XHR/fetch via headless Chromium, extract API endpoints, produce a clone scaffold + OpenAPI stub. ALWAYS writes a PDF (and a .md) to workspace/reverse/<slug>-report.pdf.

url.description: URL to analyze (must be a target you own or are authorized to inspect)

name.description: Optional slug for the report filename (default: derived from URL)

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use (used for live network capture + PDF rendering)
side_effects: writes workspace/reverse/<slug>-report.pdf and .md; outbound HTTP request to target. To show it, end the reply with `ATTACH: <pdf path>`

name: reverse.app
summary: Reverse-engineer an app/binary (cross-platform). macOS .app: Info.plist, otool -L, frameworks, entitlements. Windows/Linux .exe/.dll/ELF: format ID, framework/runtime inference, URL/API strings. ALWAYS writes a PDF (and .md) to workspace/reverse/<slug>-report.pdf.

path.description: Absolute path to a .app bundle, .exe/.dll, or binary (must be a target you own or are authorized to inspect)

name.description: Optional slug for the report filename (default: derived from basename)

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use (for PDF rendering)
macOS-only deep inspection (otool/plutil/codesign) needs macOS; off-macOS falls back to generic binary analysis

name: reverse.file
summary: Reverse-engineer any file (cross-platform, pure-JS): identify format from magic bytes, hexdump first 256 bytes, extract printable strings. ALWAYS writes a PDF (and .md) to workspace/reverse/<slug>-report.pdf.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to analyze (must be a file you own or are authorized to inspect)

name.description: Optional slug for the report filename (default: derived from basename)

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use (for PDF rendering)

name: mind
summary: Helm Mind — AI-first second brain over HelmBrain. Verbs: save, capture, find, synthesize, research, daily, recap, health. Reads the vault first and writes AI-first notes back (the vault rewrites itself).

input.description: Topic/query/conversation for the verb. Optional for daily/health/recap.

requires: HelmBrain vault present; Claude engine configured
side_effects: reads and updates notes in the HelmBrain vault
